Project log / 02AI hardware · In development
Ambient computing exploration

AI
Wearable

Exploring a quieter kind of personal AI: present when useful, physical by design and less demanding than another screen.

Working draft — details to be confirmed
Scope
Product concept, electronics, AI
Role
Independent builder
Period
Ongoing
Status
Private prototyping
On this page
  1. The question 01
  2. Concept 02
  3. Principles 03
  4. Next steps 04
01

What if an assistant felt less like an app?

Most AI interactions still begin by opening a screen, finding an interface and explicitly starting a task. A wearable could shorten that distance—but only if it earns a place in daily life.

This project investigates the hardware, interaction and privacy questions behind a compact personal assistant. The direction is intentionally open while early prototypes reveal which moments are genuinely improved by a dedicated device.

Question

Useful moments

Identify small, repeated situations where hands-free assistance has a clear advantage.

Form

Wearable hardware

Explore a physical format that is comfortable, discreet and understandable.

Interaction

Low-friction input

Test simple controls and feedback that do not require a conventional app screen.

Trust

Visible state

Make recording, processing and connectivity states easy to recognize.

02

A provisional interaction loop.

The architecture below is a working model for exploration, not a finalized implementation. It separates sensing, local device behaviour, AI services and feedback so each trade-off can be tested independently.

Conceptual signal path / draft
User inputVoice / control
Wearable deviceCapture / state
AI serviceInterpret / respond
Private feedbackAudio / haptics

Exploration areas

  • Embedded systems
  • Audio
  • AI APIs
  • Power
  • Industrial design
  • Privacy
03

Principles before features.

01

Obvious when active

People nearby and the wearer should never have to guess whether sensing is active.

02

Fast to dismiss

The interaction should end instantly and without navigating through menus.

03

Focused utility

A small set of reliable jobs is more useful than a broad but unpredictable promise.

04

Phone optional

The core moment should not depend on taking out another device.

04

What the next prototype needs to answer.

The priority is evidence, not polish: which interaction deserves dedicated hardware, what feedback feels natural, and what level of battery life makes the object viable.

Prototype

Interaction rig

Test input and feedback patterns before committing to a compact enclosure.

Document

Measured trade-offs

Add verified details on components, latency, power and user testing as the project develops.

Next project / 03

Luggie
